handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:323:### 3.5.1 Lookup-table evasion predicates (round 2 per Codex audit M6 / P1 → P0)
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:497:- A4 dual external audit (Codex + Gemini); conservative VETO>CHALLENGE>PASS; iterate to PASS/PASS before A5
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:498:- A5 commit gate: PREREG + split + notepad + audit verdicts. **No Phase B work before A5.**
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:512:- C2 **hard-10 adaptation × 2 random Boltzmann routing seeds; independent unit = per-problem (seeds = noise-reducing replicates), not (problem, seed)** (round 3 fix per Codex round-2 R3-1 — within-problem seed correlation). Hard-10 sample is drawn from the adaptation set (144 problems) using the same hash-based deterministic procedure as the three-split: `random.Random("hard10_pput_ccl_seed").sample(adaptation_set, 10)`. The 10 problem IDs + their fingerprint are pre-committed in Phase A5. Boltzmann seeds = `[31415, 2718]`. Total per-mode runs: 5 modes × 10 problems × 2 seeds = 100 jsonl rows. McNemar per condition pair uses **n = 10 per-problem paired-binary observations** (paired sign of `mean(VPPUT_seed1, VPPUT_seed2)_full > mean(VPPUT_seed1, VPPUT_seed2)_alt`). See § 9.2 + § 9.5 for power analysis.
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:518:### Phase D — CCL shadow mode (days 18-24)
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:527:  **D5 SPOF contingency (round 2 per dual audit M5)**:
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:537:**Single-touch discipline**: heldout-54 problem set is touched ONCE for an evaluation campaign. Within that single touch, the sealed eval runs `k+2` sub-evaluations on the SAME problem set; this is not multiple independent draws — it is a leave-one-out factorial design within one sample (round 2 per Codex audit M3).
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:540:- E2 **Sealed heldout evaluation campaign — leave-one-out within sealed eval (revised round 2)**:
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:550:- E4 Final dual external audit (Codex + Gemini); conservative VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS.
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:561:| B | **Trust Root immutability** (Patch B upgrade + round 2 fallback per Codex audit TR): ArchitectAI writes only to `user_space/Δ_*/`. Primary enforcement: syscall-layer `EPERM` trap. **Fallback enforcement** (if syscall layer not feasible in Rust user-space without privileged sandbox): library-level write gate at the artifact-write API + path whitelist + `panic!()` abort on bypass attempt + post-write `git diff` against Trust Root paths; any non-empty diff = `panic!("TRUST_ROOT_BYPASS")` and process termination. Either primary OR fallback enforcement passing the `tests/trust_root_immutability.rs` conformance suite is sufficient for Gate B; bypass through both layers = arc BLOCKER. The PREREG accepts the fallback as equivalent strength because the conformance test is identical: simulated ArchitectAI write attempts to Trust Root paths must abort the process. |
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:589:k_max = 10        (a priori upper bound on artifacts entering Phase E; pre-committed at A5)
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:596:- If realized k ≤ k_max: Holm uses N_max = 34 throughout. Unused slots (positions k+1..k_max) forfeit their α budget; we are conservative.
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:632:**Phase C requires 10/10 paired wins for any of H1-H4 to reject** at the smallest Holm threshold. This is a strict criterion. Implication:
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:647:| 38/54 | 2.86 | 0.00211 | NO |
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:657:Family construction (tests, directions, thresholds, k_max = 10, N_max = 34) is FROZEN at Phase A5 commit gate. No tests added post-hoc. No threshold relaxation post-hoc. No k_max increase without formal addendum + dual external re-audit.
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:659:## 10. claim language constraints (revised round 2 per dual audit M7)
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:674:### 10.1 Default claim language for negative outcomes (round 2 — partial-outcome cases added)
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:748:This file is committed FIRST, then split (§ 2.2) is generated, then dual external audit (§ A4) launched. Phase B does NOT begin before Phase A4 returns PASS/PASS. Do not modify after Phase A5 commit gate except via formal addendum (`PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26_ADDENDUM_*.md`) which itself requires dual external audit.
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:750:### 14.1 Date stamp note (round 2 per Codex audit H2)
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:752:The filename and frontmatter date `2026-04-26` reflect the date of the `PPUT_CCL_SPLITS_2026-04-26.json` generation and the `GEMINI_DEEPTHINK_FULL_PASS_2026-04-26.md` archive landing. Per the running session's `currentDate` indicator, drafting began evening of `2026-04-25`; the dual external audit + round-2 revisions were completed on the same multi-hour session, with the date stamp committed forward to `2026-04-26` to match the canonical artifact suite (split JSON, audit verdict). Both refer to the same arc launch event; the one-day forward stamp is a documentation-stability convention, not a backdating issue. The actual arc start = first commit of this PREREG round-2 + Phase A5 commit gate, which is what `committed_commit_sha` captures.
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:766:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **M1**: § 5 restructured — each H1-H4 has ONE primary endpoint (per-(problem,seed) Lean-verified Progress sign or paired VPPUT sign); H5 reclassified as deterministic gate (no α). § 5.2 added — per-artifact heldout family `4·k` tests (j-PPUT / j-FAR / j-RR / j-CPR). § 5.3 family size = `4 + 4k`. § 6 C2 independent unit clarified = (problem, seed). § 9 rewritten with Holm-Bonferroni stepwise procedure + power expectation + family construction frozen at A5. | Codex audit M1 + Gemini audit M1 (round 1 dual-CHALLENGE convergent) |
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:767:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **M2**: § 2.3 reframed — heldout sealing is **operational** (access-pattern + conformance), not cryptographic; SHA-256 is integrity check (tamper detection), not access control; new context-blacklist: PREREG + SPLITS files cannot enter agent prompt context; new tool-call pre-flight EPERM filter; residual paraphrasing risk acknowledged. | Codex audit M2 (round 1 LEAKAGE CHALLENGE — Gemini missed) |
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:768:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **M3**: § 6 E2-E3 rewritten — leave-one-out within sealed eval. `k+2` sub-evaluations on the SAME 54 problems × 3 seeds (BASELINE + ALL + k LOO). Per-artifact ΔPPUT = PPUT_all − PPUT_minus_j. Single-touch discipline preserved. Compute budget validated (~27 wall-hours at k=10, 5x headroom). | Codex audit M3 (round 1 GATE-H MEASUREMENT CHALLENGE — Gemini missed) |
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:769:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **M4**: § 7 Gate H — RR criterion relaxed from brittle `RR = 0` to per-problem one-sided exact binomial test against pre-registered baseline regression rate `p_0` (calibrated in Phase B6/B7 on adaptation set). Reachability calibrated to empirical reality. | Gemini audit M4 (round 1 GATE-H REACHABILITY CHALLENGE — Codex missed) |
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:770:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **M5**: § 6 D5 — SPOF contingency added. 48h per-candidate review SLA; SLA breach → `deferred` queue; 30-day arc clock does NOT pause for user unavailability; ≥ 5 candidates queued > 48h triggers Phase D early abort to Phase E. User keystroke logs to `human_meta_predicate_log_*.jsonl` (Phase E auto-meta-predicate Trust Data). | Gemini audit M5 + Codex audit P1 (both flagged; consolidated to P0 per conservative rule) |
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:771:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **M6**: § 3.5.1 — 4 additional content predicates (`docs_no_problem_id_keys`, `docs_no_theorem_name_keys`, `docs_rolling_hash_multi_window` at sizes [16/32/64/128], `docs_max_dict_cardinality ≤ 8`); 3-strikes-on-source-cluster ArchitectAI degenerate-mode notification. Closes lookup-table evasion via 63-token chunks / problem ID / theorem name keys / high-cardinality dicts. | Codex audit M6 (round 1 GOODHART CHALLENGE — P1 escalated to P0) |
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:772:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **M7**: § 10 rewritten — every claim row gets a "scope qualifier" column requiring `deepseek-v4-flash thinking-off` + Phase D heterogeneous specifier; § 10.1 added — partial-outcome allowed claims for 5 specific scenarios (Phase C all fail / Phase D zero Quarantined / k Quarantined zero Certified / partial PASS / rollback fail). | Codex audit M7 (round 1 CLAIM-LANG + HETEROGENEITY-TIMING scoping) |
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:773:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **TR**: § 7 Gate B + § 1.8 Trust Root — fallback enforcement spec added (lib-level write gate + path whitelist + post-write `git diff` + `panic!()` on bypass) for the case where Rust user-space cannot reach syscall-level EPERM. Either primary or fallback enforcement passing the conformance suite is sufficient for Gate B. | Codex audit TR (round 1 TRUST-ROOT-ENFORCEMENT CHALLENGE) |
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:774:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **H1**: heldout-49 → heldout-54 grep-and-replace through PREREG + notepad. Bucket-pseudocode comments updated to "nominal X / realized Y". | Codex audit H1 (round 1 REPRO PASS-with-cleanup) |
handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:775:| 2026-04-26 (round 2) | **H2**: § 14.1 added — date stamp note explaining 2026-04-25 drafting / 2026-04-26 stamp convention. | Codex audit H2 (round 1 REPRO PASS-with-cleanup) |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:8:- A0d (this doc): Trust Root manifest 20 → 24 (this commit); v2 documents the harness as constitutional artifact
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit (swarm), FC2-N20 mr tick (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof (swarm), FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic (swarm), FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (swarm, with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (verify_omega_detailed × 2 + verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:12:- A7 (post-A6): heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing — `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change (per-provider multi-key round-robin: 3 SiliconFlow keys split concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools, mitigating V3L-27 single-key N=30 collapse). `scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh` + `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` probe each of the 3 keys (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, max_tokens=8) — A7 verified all 3 keys responding 2026-04-26 (1.5–3s latency, 33+1 tokens; round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls). New `siliconflow:<model>` provider-prefix syntax in `detect_provider()` for unambiguous routing in `AGENT_MODELS` payloads (Phase D heterogeneous swarms). Memory `reference_siliconflow.md` records SiliconFlow as the heterogeneous-LLM lane (NOT a fallback target). FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI provider expansion — heterogeneous δ instances across SF catalog enable Phase D meta-loop). Trust Root manifest 28 → 31 (3 entries: `llm_proxy.py` + 2 smoke scripts).
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e5 (post-A8e4, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-5 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — same split as round-4): five narrow doc/source-text fixes; zero source-code changes. **J1** Round-4 outcome + A8e4 fixes shipped section added to the packet (was missing — Codex R5#1 caught the gap). **J2** `<pending>` commit placeholders for A8e2/A8e3/A8e4 replaced with actual SHAs (`0af47b7` / `3d38ba5` / `8693789`); A8e5 self-reference now uses "this commit" to break the placeholder-staleness pattern. **J3** packet § 6 Q6 question text reworded: "24 → 30 / 6 new entries" → "24 → 34 / 10 new entries"; full enumerated list added. **J4** TRACE_MATRIX § 1 top-bullet TR-deltas corrected: A5 `25 → 26` → `26 → 27`; A6 `26 → 27` → `27 → 28` (matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header). **J5** round-3 retrospective text about `make_pput` arg count: 21 → 24 (Gemini R5 spot-check; F1's `run_id` parameter brought the post-A8e count from 21 to 24). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (no new files; doc-only changes). All findings are documentary; both auditors confirmed the code is sound + ready for Phase B.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:17:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:61:| `make_pput` A4 args + per-call-site verifier brackets + per-tool proposal hashing (A4) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | wires the 3 fields at every emit site (oneshot + swarm OMEGA + swarm step Complete + swarm synthetic short-circuit + swarm natural max-tx exhaustion); 5 unit/conformance tests (`test_a4_decomposed_metrics_round_trip`, `test_a4_tactic_diversity_helper`, `test_a4_verifier_wait_bounded_by_total_wall_time`, `test_a4_emit_max_tx_exhaustion_row`, `test_a4_synthetic_short_circuit_does_not_set_hit_max_tx`) | ✅ |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:62:| `budget_regime::{BUDGET_REGIME_ENV_VAR, MAX_TRANSACTIONS_ENV_VAR, DEFAULT_MAX_TRANSACTIONS, BudgetRegime, BudgetError, parse_budget_regime, parse_max_transactions, effective_max_tx, resolve_budget}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs` | FC2-N22 HALT decomposition by budget regime — declares which partitioning rule (`total_proposal` / `per_agent` / `token_total` / `wall_clock`) governed the loop bound. Phase A scope = first two regimes implemented; latter two declared startup-fatal `UnimplementedRegime` so a misconfigured run aborts before consuming LLM budget. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 dependency cleared. | ✅ |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:63:| `RunAggregate::{budget_regime, budget_max_transactions}` + `PputResult::{budget_regime, budget_max_transactions}` (A5) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/jsonl_schema.rs` + `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | FC2-N22: every emitted v2 row stamps the regime label + base budget so downstream PPUT analysis can join on the partitioning rule. Loop bound at `run_swarm` startup = `resolve_budget(n_agents).effective_max_tx`; default (env unset) preserves the Phase B baseline `total_proposal × 200` bit-for-bit. 16 unit tests (15 in `budget_regime::tests` + 1 `test_a5_budget_regime_round_trip` in jsonl_schema). | ✅ |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:64:| `fc_trace::{FcId, FC_TRACE_*ENV*, fc_trace_enabled, emit_event, json_str}` (A6) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/fc_trace.rs` | meta-witness for FC1 / FC2 / FC3 path coverage. 7-variant `FcId` enum produces stable strings (`FC1-N7` / `FC1-N11` / `FC1-N12` / `FC1-E18` / `FC2-N20` / `FC2-N22` / `FC3-N31`) that Phase D consumers + TRACE_MATRIX rows join on. `FC_TRACE=1` gate cached in `OnceLock` (zero-overhead in production). 6 unit tests (label stability + JSON escape + cold-path no-op). | ✅ |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:65:| `run_id::mint_run_id` correlation key + 9 wired FC events (A6 + A8e F4) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` + `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | A6 introduced a per-run correlation id (originally named `run_corr_id`, now unified into `run_id`) anchoring all events. A8e fix F1 (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) split this out into a dedicated module + threaded the same id into `make_pput` so FC events and v2 jsonl rows share one identifier (no ms drift). Anchor sites: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit / mr tick FC2-N20 / OMEGA full-proof FC2-N22 / OMEGA per-tactic FC2-N22 / natural MaxTxExhausted FC2-N22 (with `budget_regime` payload from A5) / FC1-N12 oneshot verify (A6) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (A8e F4) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_partial` (A8e F4). End-to-end smoke `tests/fc_trace_smoke.rs` exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (forced because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read). | ✅ |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:67:| `llm_proxy.py` v4 (multi-key round-robin) + `detect_provider` `siliconflow:` prefix (A7) | `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` | FC1-N7 δ/AI provider expansion — three SiliconFlow keys form a 3-element round-robin pool keyed on `_per_key_requests[provider]`. Phase D heterogeneous swarms can address SF models via `AGENT_MODELS=siliconflow:Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct,...`. Mitigates V3L-27 (case C-027) single-key N=30 401/429 collapse documented in `cases/V3_LESSONS.md`. | ✅ |

handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:70:## § 3. Trust Root manifest expansion: 20 → 24
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:81:**Total: 24 entries** (15 from B7 + 1 B7-extra rollback_sim + 4 dual-audit fixes + 4 A0 harness). A1 (PREREG amendment) → 25; A3 (agent_models.rs) → 26; A5 (budget_regime.rs) → 27; A6 (fc_trace.rs) → 28; A7 (llm_proxy.py + smoke_siliconflow.sh + _smoke_siliconflow.py) → 31; A8e (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py) → 33; A8e2 (llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs) → 34. When B7-extra calibration eventually runs, the calibration jsonl makes 35 entries; future Phase C's `--mode` flag binary (TBD location) makes 36.

handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:103:7. ~~**Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate** — Plan agent's recommendation in N-experiments brainstorm. Phase A6 deferred; will land before Phase B (homogeneous experiments).~~ **CLOSED**: A6 (`89994c7`) shipped `fc_trace.rs` + 6 production emit sites (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`); A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (`verify_omega_detailed` alone + tape+payload retry, plus `verify_partial`), bringing the post-A8e total to **9 wired anchor sites**. Implementation chose pure stdlib over the `tracing` crate to avoid a new dep tree; the macro surface (`emit_event` + `FcId` enum) was kept small so Phase D+ can swap to a real `tracing-subscriber` bridge locally.

   224	
   225	The PREREG amendment shifted the Phase B → C gate. From the Phase A perspective, the items it lists are now satisfied:
   226

   232	
   233	## § 5. Risks and known limitations entering Phase B
   234	
   235	1. **`per_agent` budget regime untested at runtime**. A5 unit tests verify the scaling math (`base × N`) and env-coupled resolver. No live-LLM run with `BUDGET_REGIME=per_agent` has been smoked. Phase B kernel instrumentation will be the first opportunity to observe its behavior on a real problem; defer treatment to PREREG re-calibration if any anomaly surfaces.
   236	2. **FC-trace coverage still partial after A8e**. 9 wired anchor sites now cover HALT decomposition (FC2-N22 × 4 exit paths) + mr tick (FC2-N20) + Lean oracle scope (FC1-N12 × 4 sites: oneshot + swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + swarm `verify_partial`). Still NOT emitting: FC1-N7 prompt-build, FC1-N11 ∏p decision diversity (per-proposal), FC1-E18 preserve-Q_t (per ∏p=0), FC3-N31 WAL append. The `FcId` enum reserves these variants. Phase B+ kernel instrumentation should fill them in as the Phase B emit boundary lands.
   237	3. **SiliconFlow rate-limit at scale**. A7 verified 3 keys responding individually at N=1 concurrency. V3L-27 demonstrates collapse at N=30 single-key. The v4 multi-key round-robin should triple the safe N envelope but the actual sweet spot for our hetero swarm is unmeasured. Phase D heterogeneous-batch design should land a `--max-concurrency` knob (currently `LLM_PROXY_CONCURRENCY=5` env in proxy) tuned per provider.
   238	4. **Heterogeneous swarm = Phase D, not B/C**. Per F-2026-04-25-02 + the `agent_models.rs` `PHASE_D_HETERO_GATE_ENV_VAR` invariant, Phases B and C MUST stay single-model so ablation axes are not confounded. A7's plumbing exists for future Phase D work; Phase B uses the existing `deepseek-v4-flash` thinking-off backbone unchanged.
   239

   245	- (Q1.a) Each of A1–A7 was committed as one atomic commit with `FC-trace: <FC?-N?>` in the commit message, per memory `feedback_fc_first_problem_handling`. Verify by re-reading each commit message. Are any commits missing FC-trace, or claiming an FC node that doesn't match the actual code change?
   246	- (Q1.b) Did any commit touch `constitution.md`? (Per Art. V.1.1 + R-018 BLOCK, this is a violation absent sudo.) Trace each commit's file changes.
   247	- (Q1.c) Are there code changes outside the listed atoms — e.g., drift from un-committed tweaks — that should have been their own atom?
   248	
   249	### Q2 — PREREG amendment soundness (A1)
   250	- (Q2.a) ~~Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative.~~ **CLOSED A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 + A8e3 H1**: the substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT conservative. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4). Wording fixed in PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 (A8e F6) + § 8 (A8e2 G2) + this packet's A1-atom description (A8e3 H1). No further verification needed.
   251	- (Q2.b) Re-calibration conditions list 5 items (notepad ref + per-atom commits). Does this implicitly couple Phase B → C to Phase D readiness in a way that would block forward progress if Phase D slips?
   252	- (Q2.c) The amendment doc itself is in Trust Root (entry 25). Is the amendment's own SHA-256 referenced anywhere that would prevent a silent re-edit?
   253	
   254	### Q3 — Budget regime soundness (A5)
   255	- (Q3.a) `BUDGET_REGIME=per_agent` scales the loop bound as `base × N`. For a swarm at N=8 with `MAX_TRANSACTIONS=200`, the loop runs 1600 iterations — each agent receives ~200 proposals. Does this match the brainstorm § A.3 "fixed proposal budget" intent, or is a per-agent counter (each agent independently capped at 200, regardless of round-robin pickup) more faithful?
   256	- (Q3.b) `token_total` and `wall_clock` regimes are declared startup-fatal `UnimplementedRegime`. Is "fail loud" the right default, or should they fall back to `total_proposal` with a WARN log?
   257	- (Q3.c) The default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline `total_proposal × 200` bit-for-bit. Verify this is true under all code paths — including the synthetic short-circuit and error/timeout exits.

FcId enum still has 4 unwired variants (FC1-N7, FC1-N11, FC1-E18, FC3-N31) — kept as Phase B+ kernel-instrumentation work. Verify the 9-site coverage is sufficient for the round-3 acceptance bar.

    71	
    72	Per case **C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification)**: governance instrumentation is itself constitutional; tampering with rules / judge.sh / conformance tests = silent constitutional drift.

    76	| `rules/MANIFEST.sha256` (proxy for 14 rules/active/R-*.yaml) | Same pattern as cases/MANIFEST.sha256: glob hashed once, manifest tracked in Trust Root. Tampering with R-018 enforcement = "warn" silently bypasses constitution sudo gate. |
    77	| `rules/engine.py` | The interpreter of the rules. Tampering with engine.py = silent rule bypass even with intact rule files. |
    78	| `.claude/hooks/judge.sh` | The PreToolUse hook that invokes engine.py + implements R-016 fc_trace + constitution.md special-case. Tampering = bypass entire gate stack. |
    79	| `tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs` | Witness battery for TRACE_MATRIX ✅ rows. Tampering = false PASS hides drift. |

     1	// A8e2 fix G1 — recurring conformance gate for `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py`.
     2	//
     3	// Round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) caught: `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`
     4	// existed and was in Trust Root, but was only documented as a manual
     5	// invocation. A test that does not run automatically is just
     6	// documentation — it cannot prevent the V3L-27-class regression that
     7	// Gemini's round-1 VETO targeted.
     8	//
     9	// This integration test bridges the Python proxy suite into
    10	// `cargo test --workspace` so it runs on every Rust test invocation
    11	// and on every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. The
    12	// test depends on the system `python3` interpreter being available;
    13	// if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic (A8e3 fix H6 —
    14	// Codex round-3 caught the prior soft-skip behavior that masqueraded
    15	// as a passing gate when Python was missing). Explicit opt-out:
    16	// `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` (must be set deliberately;
    17	// the bypass is logged loudly to make the gate's absence visible).
    18	//
    19	// Constitutional anchor: meta-witness for atom A7 (case C-027 +
    20	// V3L-27 mitigation — multi-key round-robin avoiding single-key
    21	// rate-limit collapse).
    22	
    23	use std::path::PathBuf;
    24	use std::process::Command;
    25	
    26	fn repo_root() -> PathBuf {
    27	    // The minif2f_v4 crate's Cargo.toml lives at the repo's
    28	    // experiments/minif2f_v4 path, so two parents up is the repo root.
    29	    PathBuf::from(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"))
    30	        .parent()
    31	        .and_then(|p| p.parent())
    32	        .expect("repo root reachable from CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")
    33	        .to_path_buf()
    34	}
    35	
    36	#[test]
    37	fn proxy_python_conformance_suite_passes() {
    38	    let root = repo_root();
    39	    let script = root.join("scripts").join("test_llm_proxy.py");
    40	    assert!(
    41	        script.is_file(),
    42	        "scripts/test_llm_proxy.py must exist at the canonical path; got {:?}",
    43	        script
    44	    );
    45	
    46	    // A8e3 fix H6 (Codex R3#3): the wrapper MUST fail closed when
    47	    // `python3` is missing. A "soft skip" is exactly the silent-pass
    48	    // failure mode that Gemini's round-1 VETO targeted — a gate that
    49	    // disappears under environmental drift is not a gate. If a runner
    50	    // environment lacks Python, that's a CI configuration bug, not an
    51	    // acceptable-skip case. Explicit opt-out:
    52	    // `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` — must be set deliberately,
    53	    // never set by default. The bypass is logged loudly so the gate's
    54	    // absence is visible in test output.
    55	    let opt_out = std::env::var("SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE")
    56	        .as_deref() == Ok("1");
    57	    if opt_out {
    58	        eprintln!(
    59	            "[G1] SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1 — gate explicitly \
    60	             bypassed. This is a downgraded run; the V3L-27 round-robin \
    61	             conformance battery did NOT execute."
    62	        );
    63	        return;
    64	    }
    65	    let python_check = Command::new("python3").arg("--version").output();
    66	    assert!(
    67	        python_check.is_ok(),
    68	        "python3 not found on PATH; G1 conformance gate requires it. \
    69	         Install python3 + the openai SDK (see scripts/test_llm_proxy.py \
    70	         header) or — only with deliberate intent — set \
    71	         SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1 to bypass."
    72	    );
    73	
    74	    let output = Command::new("python3")
    75	        .arg(&script)
    76	        .current_dir(&root)
    77	        .output()
    78	        .expect("spawn python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py");
    79	
    80	    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
    81	    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);
    82	
    83	    assert!(
    84	        output.status.success(),
    85	        "scripts/test_llm_proxy.py must exit 0 (round-robin + routing \
    86	         conformance battery for V3L-27 mitigation).\n\
    87	         status: {:?}\nstdout:\n{}\nstderr:\n{}",
    88	        output.status,
    89	        stdout,
    90	        stderr
    91	    );
    92	
    93	    // Sanity-check the unittest summary line is present so a future
    94	    // refactor that removes the test class definitions surfaces here
    95	    // instead of silently skipping.
    96	    assert!(
    97	        stderr.contains("OK") || stdout.contains("OK"),
    98	        "unittest output must contain the trailing 'OK' line.\n\
    99	         stdout:\n{}\nstderr:\n{}",
   100	        stdout,
   101	        stderr
   102	    );
   103	}

handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:179:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:250:- (Q2.a) ~~Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative.~~ **CLOSED A8e F6 + A8e2 G2 + A8e3 H1**: the substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT conservative. No further verification needed.

 succeeded in 0ms:
    20	
    21	## § 2. Amendment
    22	
    23	PREREG § 5.5 calibration **DEFERRED** indefinitely with the following operative substitution for Phase B → C transition and Phase E Gate H requirements:
    24	
    25	**`p_0` for guardrail purposes**: take the **PREREG § 5.5 ceiling itself = 0.10** and use it as `p_0` directly. Any artifact j whose `j-RR` regression rate exceeds 0.10 fails Gate H per the original guardrail logic.
    26	
    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
    28	
    29	**Statistical implications** (re-stated cleanly):
    30	- No Type-I inflation. `j-RR` is a descriptive guardrail (PREREG § 5.4), not part of the inferential family — its threshold doesn't enter Holm-Bonferroni adjustment.
    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. When calibration runs (per § 3 conditions), the empirical estimate likely tightens the bar; until then we operate at the ceiling.
    32	- Acceptable trade-off because (a) Phase B and C don't gate on `j-RR` (Gate H is a Phase E artifact gate), and (b) the conditions in § 3 ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E, so the loose substitution never reaches the artifact-acceptance moment.
    33	
    34	**`genesis_payload.toml [pput_accounting_0].baseline_regression_rate`**: setting deferred to ArchitectAI commit window. Current value `0.0` is recognized as INVALID PLACEHOLDER (would auto-fail any artifact with any regression). Until calibration runs, **Gate H consumers MUST hardcode `p_0 = 0.10`** at the consumption site, not read from `genesis_payload.toml`.
    35	
    36	**`baseline_regression_jsonl_sha256`**: stays empty (calibration jsonl does not exist yet).
    37	
    38	## § 3. Conditions for re-calibration
    39	
    40	Calibration becomes worthwhile (and the deferral lifted) when ALL of:
    41	
    42	1. **N-experiments arc (Phase A-D of new plan, 2026-04-25 N-agents experiments) complete** — answers Q1/Q2/Q3 about N → PPUT, fixes (or rejects) the throttle hypothesis, sediments per-N best practices into evaluator. Without this, calibrating p_0 on a known-suboptimal N=3 swarm is calibrating against a moving baseline.
    43	
    44	2. **swarm_N=1 mode landed** (Phase A atom A2) — current `CONDITION=oneshot` is a different code path; PREREG § 5.5 ambiguous about which is the "control".
    45	
    46	3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.
    47	
    48	4. **Heterogeneous LLM agents experiment complete** (Phase A3.5 / E_hetero) — if hetero finds significant solve_rate uplift, the calibration must be done on the production model mix, not on homo n3 baseline.
    49	
    50	5. **Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists** — calibration is part of Gate H gating Phase E. Doing it before Phase D = calibrating against a counterfactual ArchitectAI that doesn't exist.
    51	
    52	When ALL 5 conditions met: re-issue PREREG_AMENDMENT to lift the deferral + trigger the 576-run batch with the (then-current) production mode.
    53	
    54	## § 4. Impact on Phase B → C transition
    55	
    56	PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
    57	
    58	Phase B → C exit checklist accordingly:
    59	- ❌ p_0 calibration jsonl frozen (was REQUIRED) → now DEFERRED with substitution per § 2
    60	- ✅ B1-B7 + B7-extra mode toggle infrastructure complete
    61	- ✅ Phase A0 harness modernization complete (post-2026-04-25 governance work)
    62	- ✅ Tools qualified (per case C-075): runner.sh, compute_p0.py, evaluator boot enforcement, etc.
    63	- ✅ Trust Root verifies clean
    64	
    65	Phase B → C dual-audit packet (next major milestone) must reference this amendment + show that Phase E Gate H consumer hardcodes `p_0 = 0.10`.
    66	
    67	## § 5. What this amendment does NOT change
    68	
    69	- **PREREG § 5.5 protocol itself** — the calibration *protocol* (288 control + 288 treatment paired runs, max-over-seeds, etc.) remains the agreed-upon procedure for IF calibration ever runs. Amendment defers the SCHEDULING, not the SCIENCE.
    70	- **PREREG § 1.8 Trust Root composition** — manifest entries unchanged by this amendment (this amendment doc is added per § 7 below).
    71	- **PREREG § 5.4 j-RR ≤ p_0 guardrail logic** — Gate H still uses the guardrail; just the p_0 source changes (hardcoded 0.10 instead of calibrated value).
    72	- **PREREG § 5.6 family total / N_max** — unchanged.
    73	- **All other PREREG § sections** — unchanged.
    74	
    75	## § 6. PREREG document treatment
    76	
    77	`PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` itself is **NOT EDITED** by this amendment. It remains the immutable round-4 frozen pre-registration. This amendment is a separate document referenced from § 5.5 forward via a pointer added to Trust Root manifest.
    78	
    79	This pattern is per CLAUDE.md "Common Law": amendments are recorded as separate cases / docs that supersede specific sections, leaving the original frozen for reproducibility. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md SHA-256 in Trust Root manifest UNCHANGED.
    80	
    81	## § 7. Trust Root impact
    82	
    83	Add this amendment doc to genesis_payload.toml [trust_root]:
    84	```
    85	"handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md" = "<sha256>"
    86	```
    87	
    88	Manifest size: 24 → 25 entries.
    89	
    90	## § 8. Audit requirement
    91	
    92	Per case C-073 ArchitectAI commit workflow: this amendment requires dual audit (Codex + Gemini, conservative VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS) before commit lands. Audit packet should specifically test:
    93	
    94	- Does the amendment violate any PREREG § 5.5 constraint? (Should not — defer is operationally permitted given § 5.5 ceiling.)
    95	- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, accepted because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first.)
    96	- Does deferral leave any phase blocked indefinitely? (Should not — § 3 lists explicit re-calibration conditions; if those never met, Phase E proceeds with the operationally-permitted ceiling substitution per § 2 final paragraph.)
    97	
    98	## § 9. Cross-references
    99	
   100	- `PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md` § 5.5 (the amended section, IMMUTABLE)
   101	- `cases/C-073_architect_ai_commit_authority.yaml` (governance basis)
   102	- `cases/C-075_do_178c_tool_qualification.yaml` (tool-readiness as re-calibration precondition)
   103	- `handover/architect-insights/B7_EXTRA_ABSTRACTION_DEPTH_FINDINGS_2026-04-25.md` (context: cost asymmetry concern)
   104	- `handover/architect-insights/THESIS_V2_GROUND_TRUTH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md` (context: ground-truth feedback discipline)
   105	- `handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md` (PASS verdict on round-4 batch — but batch was 3-7 days not 8h, motivating this deferral)

WHY DEFER

PREREG (5.5 estimated 576-run calibration as 8 hours, $3-5. Empirical
observation 2026-04-25 (during launched batch 650caf7 era):
- Per-run wall-clock ~15-30 min (was estimated ~50s) — 30-40x off
- Total batch 3-7 days realistic (was estimated 8h) — 9-21x off
- Cost still cheap (~$15-20)

The 8h estimate was based on chat-oneshot speed assumption that

== 7f4bc0c ==
7f4bc0c A3: per-agent AGENT_MODELS env var (Phase B+C single-model gate)
A3: per-agent AGENT_MODELS env var (Phase B+C single-model gate)

FC-trace: FC1-N7 (δ/AI canonical identity) + Phase B+C single-model
          invariant (notepad F-2026-04-25-02 + memory feedback_phased_checkpoint)

Phase A atom A3 — capability for binding distinct δ per Agent_i in the
swarm path. Default behavior (env unset) broadcasts the global model
from ACTIVE_MODEL to every agent_idx, preserving Phase B current
behavior bit-for-bit. Heterogeneous payloads (≥2 distinct models in
the resolved vector) are gated by PHASE_D_HETERO_OK=1; without the
gate the resolver aborts at startup before any LLM call.

NEW MODULE
- experiments/minif2f_v4/src/agent_models.rs:

  - expand_agent_models(parsed, global, n_agents, hetero_gated) — pure
    validator + expander; broadcast/positional + Phase B/C gate

== a5c78e4 ==
a5c78e4 A4: decomposed metrics (hit_max_tx + tactic_diversity + verifier_wait_ms)
A4: decomposed metrics (hit_max_tx + tactic_diversity + verifier_wait_ms)

Phase A engineering atom 4 of 8 — per Codex/Gemini N-agents brainstorms 2026-04-25:
PPUT alone conflates solve_rate × tokens_per_solve × time_per_solve. Decompose at the
emit boundary so downstream analysis can split the three components and surface
mechanism-level effects (Amdahl/USL serial-vs-parallel, search-party diversity,
budget-exhaustion vs accept).

Three new non-Optional v2 fields on RunAggregate + PputResult:

- hit_max_tx (bool): true iff the run reached max_transactions WITHOUT OMEGA AND
  without firing the B7-extra synthetic short-circuit. Disambiguates natural budget
  exhaustion (FC2-N22 HALT via MaxTxExhausted) from OMEGA accept at the same
  tx_count and from the calibration-treatment short-circuit (which exits ~150 tx
  EARLY at the rollback threshold; tagged via synthetic_short_circuit instead).

- tactic_diversity (f64 ∈ [0, 1]): distinct-payload-hash / total over every parsed

== 30f2a14 ==
30f2a14 A5: per-agent budget normalization (BUDGET_REGIME + MAX_TRANSACTIONS env vars)
A5: per-agent budget normalization (BUDGET_REGIME + MAX_TRANSACTIONS env vars)

FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (delta
instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime).

PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer 2026-04-25 sec 3 condition 3 satisfied: every
emitted v2 row now stamps the budget partitioning rule + base, so
MaxTxExhausted rows are unambiguous across N values when calibration
eventually runs. Default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline
(total_proposal x 200) bit-for-bit.

New module experiments/minif2f_v4/src/budget_regime.rs:
- BUDGET_REGIME env var (total_proposal | per_agent | token_total |
  wall_clock); default total_proposal.
- MAX_TRANSACTIONS env var; default 200.
- 4-variant enum + pure parse_budget_regime / parse_max_transactions /
  effective_max_tx + env-coupled resolve_budget.

A6: per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events (fc_trace module)

before A7/A8).

Codex N-agents brainstorm 2026-04-25 sec D recommended OpenTelemetry-
style structured JSONL events tagged with FC IDs so Phase D ArchitectAI
can replay the constitutional path without source access. Implementation
chose pure stdlib over the tracing crate to avoid a new dep tree; the
emit_event + FcId surface is small enough that Phase D+ can swap to a
tracing-subscriber bridge locally.

  consumers + TRACE_MATRIX rows join on.

A7: SiliconFlow heterogeneous-LLM provider plumbing (proxy + 3-key smoke)

User correction 2026-04-26: SiliconFlow is the project's heterogeneous-
LLM lane, NOT a fallback / probe-only target. Three keys provided for
high-concurrency multi-key round-robin. Memory reference_siliconflow.md
records this lesson (context-loss anti-pattern: check .env + project
files BEFORE asking the user where credentials live).

Plumbing brought into v4 from v3:
- src/drivers/llm_proxy.py — OpenAI-compatible local HTTP server with
  one load-bearing v4 change vs v3 fork: per-provider multi-key
  round-robin. PROVIDERS map now holds a list of env-var names per
  provider; clients_by_provider builds one OpenAI client per available
  key; get_client_round_robin distributes via _rr_counters mod len.

  1486	    runtime_accepted: bool, post_hoc_verified: bool, start: Instant,
  1487	    gp_tokens: u64, gp_nodes: usize, tx_count: u64,
  1488	    tool_dist: Option<HashMap<String, u32>>,
  1489	    unique_payload_ratio: Option<f64>,
  1490	    gp_payload: Option<String>,
  1491	    gp_path: Option<String>,
  1492	    gp_proof_file: Option<String>,
  1493	    total_run_token_count: Option<u64>,
  1494	    failed_branch_count: Option<u32>,
  1495	    total_wall_time_ms: Option<u64>,
  1496	    // Phase A atom A4 (decomposed metrics). All callers must pass
  1497	    // explicit values — the v2 fields are non-Optional.
  1498	    hit_max_tx: bool,
  1499	    distinct_proposals: u64,
  1500	    total_proposals: u64,
  1501	    verifier_wait_ms: u64,
  1502	    // Phase A atom A5 (FC2-N22 budget regime stamp). Caller declares
  1503	    // the regime + base BEFORE the loop so MaxTxExhausted rows are
  1504	    // unambiguous about which partitioning rule produced them.
  1505	    budget_regime: minif2f_v4::budget_regime::BudgetRegime,
  1506	    budget_max_transactions: u32,
  1507	    // A8e fix F1 (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4): run_id minted by caller (run_swarm
  1508	    // or run_oneshot) at function entry; passed in here so the v2 jsonl
  1509	    // row stamps the SAME identifier the FC events used. No more ms drift.
  1510	    run_id: &str,
  1511	) -> PputResult {
  1512	    // PPUT-CCL Phase B B4 (mid-term audit P0-A fix 2026-04-25):
  1513	    // make_pput is now PURELY computational. The caller MUST decide both
  1514	    // `runtime_accepted` (did the evaluator's runtime gate fire?) and
  1515	    // `post_hoc_verified` (did Lean independently confirm the proof?). The
  1516	    // prior implementation derived `post_hoc_verified = has_gp` internally,
  1517	    // which would have laundered Phase C Soft Law fake-accepts into the
  1518	    // North Star pput_verified. Forcing the caller to pass both legs makes
  1519	    // Soft Law's design point unmissable: any caller that fakes runtime
  1520	    // accept must explicitly pass post_hoc_verified=verify_post_hoc(...)
  1521	    // or the divergence will surface immediately.
  1522	    //
  1523	    // Phase B all callers pass `(runtime_accepted, post_hoc_verified) = (X, X)`
  1524	    // because runtime IS Lean today. Phase C diverges at the Soft Law
  1525	    // mode call site, not inside this function.
  1526	    let has_gp = runtime_accepted; // legacy `has_golden_path` field semantics
  1527	    let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
  1528	    let pput = if has_gp && elapsed > 0.0 { 100.0 / elapsed } else { 0.0 };
  1529	    // C-012 provenance: populated from env vars; None when unset (backward compat).
  1530	    let build_sha = std::env::var("BUILD_SHA").ok();
  1531	    let classifier_version = std::env::var("CLASSIFIER_VERSION").ok();
  1532	    let boltzmann_seed = std::env::var("BOLTZMANN_SEED")
  1533	        .ok().and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());
  1534	
  1535	    // Mid-term audit P0-B fix 2026-04-25: collapse Optional accumulator/clock
  1536	    // values into required v2 fields. Phase B always has values for these
  1537	    // (B2 + B3 wire them at every emit site); the prior Option wrapping was
  1538	    // overly defensive and let the v2 schema slip from the contract.
  1539	    let c_i = total_run_token_count.unwrap_or(0);
  1540	    let t_i = total_wall_time_ms.unwrap_or(0);
  1541	    let failed_count = failed_branch_count.unwrap_or(0);
  1542	
  1543	    let progress_runtime = compute_progress_runtime(runtime_accepted);
  1544	    let progress_verified =
  1545	        compute_progress_verified(runtime_accepted, post_hoc_verified);

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""
     3	LLM Proxy v4 — OpenAI-compatible local HTTP server with token metering.
     4	
     5	Phase A atom A7. Adapted from v3's `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` with one
     6	load-bearing v4 change: per-provider multi-key round-robin so the three
     7	SiliconFlow keys (SILICONFLOW_API_KEY / _SECONDARY / _TERTIARY) split
     8	concurrent traffic across separate rate-limit pools — the V3L-27
     9	N=30 → 401/429 collapse documented in `cases/V3_LESSONS.md` was
    10	single-key. The same pattern extends to other providers if multiple
    11	keys are configured.
    12	
    13	Endpoints:
    14	  POST /v1/chat/completions  (OpenAI-compatible, forwards to cloud APIs)
    15	  GET  /health
    16	  GET  /stats               (token counters + per-key request distribution)
    17	  POST /stats/reset         (reset counters — call before each experiment)
    18	
    19	Usage:
    20	  SILICONFLOW_API_KEY=sk-xxx \\
    21	  SILICONFLOW_API_KEY_SECONDARY=sk-yyy \\
    22	  SILICONFLOW_API_KEY_TERTIARY=sk-zzz \\
    23	    python3 src/drivers/llm_proxy.py --port 8080
    24	
    25	Without --provider, model identity drives routing:
    26	  - "deepseek-*" → deepseek
    27	  - "Qwen/...", "openai/...", anything containing "/" → siliconflow
    28	  - else → dashscope
    29	"""
    30	import os, sys, json, logging, argparse, time, threading, itertools
    31	from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler
    32	from socketserver import ThreadingMixIn
    33	from openai import OpenAI, RateLimitError, APIStatusError
    34	
    35	logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(message)s')
    36	log = logging.getLogger("llm_proxy")
    37	
    38	# Each provider entry: (base_url, [env-var names tried in order]).
    39	# Multiple env names = multi-key round-robin. The PRIMARY name MUST be
    40	# first; any later names are optional fallback / additional pool keys.
    41	PROVIDERS = {
    42	    "dashscope": (
    43	        "https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1",
    44	        ["DASHSCOPE_API_KEY"],
    45	    ),
    46	    "aliyun": (
    47	        "https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1",
    48	        ["DASHSCOPE_API_KEY"],
    49	    ),
    50	    "siliconflow": (
    51	        "https://api.siliconflow.cn/v1",
    52	        [
    53	            "SILICONFLOW_API_KEY",
    54	            "SILICONFLOW_API_KEY_SECONDARY",
    55	            "SILICONFLOW_API_KEY_TERTIARY",
    56	        ],
    57	    ),
    58	    "deepseek": (
    59	        "https://api.deepseek.com",
    60	        ["DEEPSEEK_API_KEY"],
    61	    ),
    62	    "volcengine": (
    63	        "https://ark.cn-beijing.volces.com/api/v3",
    64	        ["VOLCENGINE_API_KEY"],
    65	    ),
    66	    "nvidia": (
    67	        "https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1",
    68	        ["NVIDIA_NIM_API_KEY"],
    69	    ),
    70	}
    71	
    72	# Per-(provider, key-index) OpenAI client cache: provider -> list[OpenAI]
    73	clients_by_provider = {}
    74	# Round-robin counter per provider.
    75	_rr_counters = {}
    76	_rr_lock = threading.Lock()
    77	# Per-key request counters for /stats observability.
    78	_per_key_requests = {}  # provider -> list[int]
    79	
    80	
    81	def _build_clients(provider):
    82	    """Return list of OpenAI clients for `provider`, one per available key.
    83	
    84	    Lazy. Caches in `clients_by_provider`. Raises ValueError if NO key
    85	    is set for the provider.
    86	    """
    87	    if provider in clients_by_provider:
    88	        return clients_by_provider[provider]
    89	    base_url, key_envs = PROVIDERS[provider]
    90	    keys = []
    91	    for env_name in key_envs:
    92	        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
    93	        if v:
    94	            keys.append((env_name, v))
    95	    if not keys:
    96	        raise ValueError(
    97	            f"No keys set for provider={provider}; tried env vars {key_envs}"
    98	        )
    99	    clients = [OpenAI(api_key=k, base_url=base_url) for (_, k) in keys]
   100	    clients_by_provider[provider] = clients
   101	    _per_key_requests[provider] = [0] * len(clients)
   102	    log.info(
   103	        f"[provider {provider}] resolved {len(clients)} key(s) from envs: "
   104	        f"{[name for (name, _) in keys]}"
   105	    )
   106	    return clients
   107	
   108	
   109	def get_client_round_robin(provider):
   110	    """Return (client, key_index) using round-robin across configured keys."""
   111	    clients = _build_clients(provider)
   112	    with _rr_lock:
   113	        idx = _rr_counters.get(provider, 0) % len(clients)
   114	        _rr_counters[provider] = idx + 1
   115	        _per_key_requests[provider][idx] += 1
   116	    return clients[idx], idx
   117	
   118	
   119	# ── Token Metering ──
   120	_stats_lock = threading.Lock()
   121	_stats = {
   122	    "prompt_tokens": 0,
   123	    "completion_tokens": 0,
   124	    "total_tokens": 0,
   125	    "requests": 0,
   126	    "errors": 0,
   127	    "retries_429": 0,
   128	    "estimated_count": 0,
   129	}
   130	
   131	
   132	def _record_usage(prompt_tokens, completion_tokens):
   133	    with _stats_lock:
   134	        _stats["prompt_tokens"] += prompt_tokens
   135	        _stats["completion_tokens"] += completion_tokens
   136	        _stats["total_tokens"] += prompt_tokens + completion_tokens
   137	        _stats["requests"] += 1
   138	
   139	
   140	def _record_estimated():
   141	    with _stats_lock:
   142	        _stats["estimated_count"] += 1
   143	
   144	
   145	def _record_error():
   146	    with _stats_lock:
   147	        _stats["errors"] += 1
   148	
   149	
   150	def _record_retry():
   151	    with _stats_lock:
   152	        _stats["retries_429"] += 1
   153	
   154	
   155	def _reset_stats():
   156	    with _stats_lock:
   157	        for k in _stats:
   158	            _stats[k] = 0
   159	        for prov in _per_key_requests:
   160	            _per_key_requests[prov] = [0] * len(_per_key_requests[prov])
   161	
   162	
   163	def _get_stats():
   164	    with _stats_lock:
   165	        snap = dict(_stats)
   166	        snap["per_key_requests"] = {
   167	            p: list(v) for p, v in _per_key_requests.items()
   168	        }
   169	        return snap
   170	
   171	
   172	# ── Rate Limiter ──
   173	_rate_lock = threading.Lock()
   174	_rate_semaphore = threading.Semaphore(int(os.environ.get("LLM_PROXY_CONCURRENCY", "5")))
   175	_cooldown_until = 0.0
   176	
   177	
   178	def _wait_for_cooldown():
   179	    global _cooldown_until
   180	    now = time.time()
   181	    if now < _cooldown_until:
   182	        wait = _cooldown_until - now
   183	        log.info(f"[RATE LIMITER] Cooling down {wait:.1f}s")
   184	        time.sleep(wait)
   185	
   186	
   187	def _trigger_cooldown(seconds):
   188	    global _cooldown_until
   189	    with _rate_lock:
   190	        new_until = time.time() + seconds
   191	        if new_until > _cooldown_until:
   192	            _cooldown_until = new_until
   193	            log.warning(f"[RATE LIMITER] Global cooldown {seconds}s")
   194	
   195	
   196	def detect_provider(model):
   197	    """Route by model identifier. v4 prefers explicit `provider:model`
   198	    syntax (e.g. `siliconflow:Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct`); falls back
   199	    to model-string heuristics for backward compat with v3 callers.
   200	
   201	    Routing matrix (A8e fix F3, Codex#4):
   202	      explicit `provider:...`            → that provider (if known)
   203	      contains "deepseek"                → deepseek
   204	      slash-separated id (huggingface-style "Org/Model")
   205	                                          → siliconflow (dispatches the
   206	                                            full catalog including Qwen,
   207	                                            openai-compat, Meta, etc.)
   208	      bare "qwen3-*" / "qwen-*"          → dashscope (Aliyun Qwen direct)
   209	      else                                → dashscope (default fallback)
   210	
   211	    Round-1 audit caught a routing inversion: `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct`
   212	    used to misroute to dashscope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won
   213	    after the slash check. The slash-form is now the FIRST heuristic
   214	    (after explicit prefix + deepseek substring), so any HuggingFace-
   215	    style id routes to siliconflow as the catalog provider.
   216	    """
   217	    if ":" in model:
   218	        prefix = model.split(":", 1)[0].lower()
   219	        if prefix in PROVIDERS:
   220	            return prefix

   217	    if ":" in model:

   221	    m = model.lower()
   222	    if "deepseek" in m:

   224	    if "/" in model:

   226	        # the heterogeneous catalog provider.

   228	    if m.startswith("qwen"):
   229	        # Bare qwen3-*, qwen-* without slash = direct DashScope catalog.

   231	    return "dashscope"
   232	
   233	
   234	def strip_provider_prefix(model):
   235	    """If model is `provider:foo/bar`, return `foo/bar`; else `model`."""
   236	    if ":" in model:
   237	        prefix, rest = model.split(":", 1)
   238	        if prefix.lower() in PROVIDERS:
   239	            return rest
   240	    return model

**Findings**

1. **A8/RQ14 is not actually satisfied.** The packet says the only acceptable remaining `<pending>` matches are inside the Round-5 outcome paragraph, but the current file also has `<pending>` in the Round-6 J2 bullet and in RQ14 itself. That makes the round-6 closure criterion false as written.  
Cites: [A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:100), [line 108](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:108), [line 115](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:115).

2. **A7 routing-conformance closure is still incomplete for slash-form DeepSeek catalog IDs.** The packet claims “slash-form ⇒ `siliconflow` FIRST,” and Q5.a frames slash IDs as SiliconFlow routing, but `detect_provider()` checks `"deepseek"` before `/`. A HuggingFace-style model such as `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` routes to `deepseek`, not `siliconflow`. The conformance tests cover bare DeepSeek and several non-DeepSeek slash IDs, but not this collision. Either make slash-form truly win, or document/test DeepSeek-slash as an intentional exception.  
Cites: [A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:19), [line 266](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:266), [llm_proxy.py](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/drivers/llm_proxy.py:201), [line 222](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/drivers/llm_proxy.py:222), [line 224](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/drivers/llm_proxy.py:224), [test_llm_proxy.py](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/scripts/test_llm_proxy.py:47), [line 65](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/scripts/test_llm_proxy.py:65).

3. **TRACE_MATRIX still contains the stale `make_pput` “21 positional args” statement.** A8e5 J5 says this was corrected to 24, and the packet note now says 24, but TRACE_MATRIX’s A8e3 retrospective still says “signature is now 21 positional args.” The code signature includes the post-F1 `run_id` parameter and is 24 positional args.  
Cites: [TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13), [line 15](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15), [A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:76), [evaluator.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:1484).
